text.title: GraphQL API Design Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed in shipped api-graphql work. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Query for 100 orders triggers 100 user lookups; p95 jumps from 80ms to 1.5s.
antipattern.fix: Wrap relation in DataLoader; batch+cache per request. See rule dataloader-on-1-to-n.

antipattern.symptom: Attacker sends nested query 25 levels deep; gateway OOMs.
antipattern.root-cause: Production accepts arbitrary query strings + no depth cap.
antipattern.fix: Persisted-query allowlist + depth/complexity caps. See rules persisted-queries-only-in-prod + depth-and-complexity-caps.

antipattern.symptom: Server can no longer add an optional field without a major version bump because every field is non-null.
antipattern.fix: Nullable by default; non-null only with explicit use-case justification. See rule no-overfetch-by-default.

antipattern.symptom: Mobile client has two error parsers (REST + GraphQL); bugs accumulate on the GraphQL path.
antipattern.root-cause: Author used Apollo default error shape; never aligned with REST.
antipattern.fix: Wrap formatError to emit extensions.problem with RFC 7807 fields. See rule error-extensions-rfc-7807.
